XRD - what it found: Pattern and fwhm essentially unchanged up to 5 MGy - crystallinity fully retained

XRD - what it found: fwhm shows only small fluctuation to 5 MGy - crystallinity essentially retained
FESEM - what it found: Irregular-sphere morphology unchanged; crystal size decreased slightly at 4-5 MGy

Stability: Good to 4 MGy, degrades at 5 MGy

Dose: Decomposes from 1 MGy; pore structure gone by 2 MGy

XRD - what it found: Peak at 12 deg loses intensity; first two peaks broaden ONLY after chronic dosing; acute leaves pattern intact
FESEM - what it found: Uniform under both rates, but visible crystallite-surface roughening after chronic dosing only
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Dose-RATE sensitive, not just dose-sensitive: same total dose delivered slowly causes far more damage than delivered quickly

XRD - what it found: Bragg peaks broaden/lose intensity above 1.25 MGy - loss of long-range order

XRD - what it found: No collapse of diffraction pattern detected up to 2 MGy
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Gamma radiolysis removes extra-framework unreacted linker trapped in pores, raising measured BET rather than lowering it

XRD - what it found: No significant structural distortion detected
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Only 11% pore-volume loss at highest dose tested; one of the more stable Al-MOFs

XRD - what it found: Dose-dependent sensitivity trend similar to (but less severe than) ZIF-8
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Despite Zr(IV) being a hard Lewis acid, still shows measurable dose-dependent degradation

XRD - what it found: Quick decomposition of diffraction pattern at elevated dose
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Divalent Cu(II), a softer Lewis acid, forms weaker M-carboxylate bonds - fastest decomposition among carboxylate MOFs tested

XRD - what it found: Progressive Bragg intensity loss/broadening; non-isokinetic amorphization via inhomogeneous nucleation
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Beam-induced amorphization within minutes-hours; rate accelerates with temperature even below thermal decomposition limit

XRD - what it found: Same inhomogeneous-nucleation amorphization at intermediate rate
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Same amorphization mechanism as ZIF-4/zni, kinetics governed by framework density and X-ray energy

XRD - what it found: Most resistant of the three to beam-induced amorphization
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Denser packing correlates with slower amorphization; beam energy is first-order variable (20 keV faster than 29 keV)

XRD - what it found: Rietveld PXRD pattern essentially unchanged/retained after 50 kGy of either beta or gamma
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Pillared-square-grid topology survives fully intact at 50 kGy, attributed to strong M-N(pyrazine) bond and radiolysis-resistant SiF6 pillar

XRD - what it found: More pronounced peak intensity/crystallinity loss than Cu analogue
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Zn-N coordination weaker than Cu-N, giving worse radiation tolerance

XRD - what it found: Visible crystallinity degradation by highest dose
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Degradation trend similar to Co/Fe analogues; all less stable than Cu

XRD - what it found: Visible crystallinity degradation
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Comparable PXRD degradation to Ni/Fe analogues

XRD - what it found: Visible crystallinity degradation
Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Overall stability order from PXRD: Cu >> Zn approx Ni approx Co approx Fe

XRD - what it found: Electron diffraction shows anisotropic lattice contraction preceding amorphization

Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: Two nonclassical damage mechanisms: (1) reversible radiolysis at low dose, (2) radiolysis-enhanced knock-on at higher dose (irreversible); damage is spatially heterogeneous

XRD - what it found: Earliest onset of peak broadening/crystallinity loss of the whole M-UiO-66 series

Main Findings / Degradation Mechanism: XRD/DRIFTS/Raman agree Ce-UiO-66 loses crystallinity fastest, consistent with weakest M-carboxylate bond (Hf>Zr>Th>Ce electronegativity trend)

XRD - what it found: Least peak broadening of the gamma-irradiated series

Dose: Gamma to 3 MGy; He-ion to 85 MGy
